# Contract: Federal Reader FFI (UniFFI Swift boundary)

**Feature**: `006-federal-cc-parser` | **Date**: 2026-07-16
**Module**: `kaname-core` (Rust) → `KanameCore` (Swift, generated by UniFFI)

Additive to the P1 + ICICI + HDFC + SBI + Yes `engine-ffi` contracts. The "external interface" is the
exported UniFFI surface (not an HTTP API); generated Swift signatures are the consumer-facing form.
**Reuses** the existing `Decimal`/`NaiveDate` custom types, the `Direction` enum, and the
`ParsedStatement`/`ParsedTransaction` records **unchanged** — Federal adds **two functions and no new
type**.

---

## Exported functions

### `read_federal_statement`

| | |
|-|-|
| Rust | `#[uniffi::export] pub fn read_federal_statement(lines: Vec<String>, full_text: String) -> ParsedStatement` |
| Swift | `public func readFederalStatement(lines: [String], fullText: String) -> ParsedStatement` |

**Contract**
- Parses **already-extracted** text `lines` (one statement row per element) + `full_text` (used only
  for enrichment). **Never** opens a file or PDF (FR-016).
- **Single layout**: `DD-MM-YYYY<sep>HH:MM <description> [+]₹<amount>` — a day-first date joined to an
  `HH:MM` time by a **single separator character** (the middle dot U+00B7, matched encoding-robustly as
  any single char, FR-004), the description, an optional leading `+`, the rupee glyph `₹` (U+20B9), and
  the amount. Produces one `ParsedTransaction` per matching row; non-matching lines
  (headers/summaries/balances/totals) are ignored (FR-007); rows matching the shape but failing
  date/amount parse go to `ParsedStatement.errored_lines` (FR-015) — **never throws, never aborts**. A
  statement with no matching rows returns empty `lines` with no error.
- **The `HH:MM` time is structural only** — it never appears in `value_date` or `description_raw`
  (FR-005).
- **Direction from Scapia's leading `+`, else the description language** — a leading `+` immediately
  before the amount → `.credit`; otherwise `classify(description, nil, nil)` (credit words → `.credit`;
  else `.debit`). **Never** the amount's sign or magnitude; a large or "negative-looking" amount does
  not change direction (FR-010/011, SC-004). (Federal has **no** `Dr`/`Cr` column.)
- **Amount** is an exact non-negative `Decimal` with scale preserved; the rupee glyph, any leading `+`,
  and Indian thousands grouping are stripped (`+₹324.45 → 324.45`, `₹2,353.13 → 2353.13`) (FR-008/009,
  SC-006). Money never crosses as `f64`.
- Enriches `period_start` + `period_end` (both parsed from the space-stripped `DDMonYYYY-DDMonYYYY`
  cycle via `%d%b%Y`) and `card_last4` (from a **fully-masked** PAN via the **un-anchored**
  `find_last4(full_text, nil)`) from `full_text` when present; absent → left `nil`.
  `XXXXXXXXXXXX4836` → `cardLast4 == "4836"`; no masked card present → `nil` (never fabricated)
  (FR-012/013/014).
- **Pure & deterministic**: identical input ⇒ identical output; no network, clock, locale, or global
  state (FR-016/017). Total function — no failure surfaced to Swift.

**Consumer expectations (app/test)**
- Call with PDFKit-extracted lines + full text. Treat `errored_lines` as review items; still consume
  every `lines` entry. Do not infer direction from `amount` — use `direction`. Treat `cardLast4 == nil`
  as "unknown", never as a value to fabricate.

### `federal_claims`

| | |
|-|-|
| Rust | `#[uniffi::export] pub fn federal_claims(full_text: String) -> bool` |
| Swift | `public func federalClaims(fullText: String) -> Bool` |

**Contract**
- Returns `true` iff `full_text` contains a Federal/Scapia issuer marker (`"Scapia"` or `"Federal
  Bank"`, case-insensitive) — the document-plausibility gate (FR-001).
- MUST return `false` for other issuers' statements (e.g. an ICICI, HDFC, SBI, or Yes document) so a
  statement is never misattributed to Federal (FR-002, US1-AC4). Pure, deterministic, no I/O.

> Internally: `read_federal_statement` wraps `read_lines(&FederalReader, …)` (single layout — **not**
> the HDFC composite); `federal_claims` delegates to `claims(&FederalReader, …, "FEDERAL")`. `lib.rs`
> re-exports both functions (as it does the ICICI/HDFC/SBI/Yes pairs); `statement/mod.rs` gains
> `pub mod federal;`.

---

## Types (all reused — no new type)

- `record ParsedStatement` / `record ParsedTransaction` — **unchanged** from the ICICI/HDFC/SBI/Yes
  slices. Federal **populates `periodStart`** (like HDFC/SBI/Yes) and `cardLast4 == "4836"`.
- `enum Direction { Debit, Credit }` → Swift `.debit` / `.credit` (P1, `uniffi::Enum`).
- Custom types `Decimal`↔`String`↔`Foundation.Decimal` and `NaiveDate`↔ISO-8601 `String`
  (P1, in `ffi.rs` + `uniffi.toml`) — **unchanged**. Money never crosses as `f64`.

> Swift shape (for reference, unchanged): `ParsedStatement { bankCode: String; lines:
> [ParsedTransaction]; erroredLines: [String]; periodStart: String?; periodEnd: String?;
> cardLast4: String?; confidence: Double }`.

---

## Contract tests (test-first — FR-023/025)

**Rust (`cargo test`)** — engine-side truth (`tests/parity.rs` + `federal.rs` unit tests):
- **Golden parity**: `read_federal_statement(basic.lines, basic.full_text)` equals the fixture's
  `expected` exactly — rows
  `2026-04-29 / 324.45 / Credit / INR / "Billpayment Payment"` and
  `2026-04-24 / 2353.13 / Debit / INR / "ExampleMerchantTokyo"`;
  `period_start 2026-04-20`; `period_end 2026-05-19`; **`card_last4 "4836"`**; `errored_lines` empty
  (SC-001/003/010).
- **Determinism**: two calls on the same input are equal.
- **Wrong issuer**: `federal_claims("ICICI Bank …") == false`, `federal_claims("HDFC Bank Credit
  Cards") == false`, `federal_claims("GSTIN of SBI Card") == false`, `federal_claims("YES BANK …") ==
  false`; `federal_claims(basic.full_text) == true` (FR-002, SC-002).
- **Direction**: the `+` row → Credit even though `Billpayment Payment` is not a credit phrase; a
  no-`+` ordinary spend → Debit; a no-`+` `refund`/`reversal`/`cashback`/`payment received` row →
  Credit via the shared classifier (SC-004).
- **Encoding-robust separator**: the same row matches with the date/time separator rendered as `·`, a
  plain space, or `.` (SC-005), and the `HH:MM` never leaks into the description.
- **Malformed row**: a line matching the Federal shape but with an unparseable date lands in
  `errored_lines`, valid rows still returned, no panic (reuses the existing seam behaviour).

**Swift (Swift Testing, `import KanameCore`)** — "core ↔ Swift Federal parse":
- `readFederalStatement(...)` over the two golden lines → 2 `lines`; `lines[0]` = `2026-04-29`,
  `Decimal(string:"324.45")`, `.credit`, `"INR"`, `"Billpayment Payment"`;
  `lines[1]` = `2026-04-24`, `Decimal(string:"2353.13")`, `.debit`, `"INR"`, `"ExampleMerchantTokyo"`.
- `periodStart == "2026-04-20"`; `periodEnd == "2026-05-19"`; **`cardLast4 == "4836"`**.
- `federalClaims(fullText:) == true` for the Federal text and `false` for an ICICI/HDFC/SBI/Yes string.
- Amounts compared as `Foundation.Decimal` value-equality (exact; never float).

---

## Stability / compatibility
- Adding two functions is additive; reusing the records means **no wire-format change** and no new
  Swift type.
- The generated `Generated/kaname_core.swift` + `KanameCoreFFI.xcframework` are build artifacts,
  rebuilt by `make core-xcframework` (which runs **before** `tuist generate`). **This contract**, not
  the generated files, is the reviewed source of truth.
